base.positive: Preserve the main subject(s) strictly exactly as in the input image view. Do not change object pose or orientation in any way; NEVER. Do not tilt, rotate, flip, or skew the object, even slightly; keep the same perspective and camera angle as the input. Preserve exact proportions and silhouette; do not change shape or curvature. Preserve all parts and attachments: branches, handles, rings, jewelry, straps, lids, and any secondary elements; do not remove or thin them. Strong shadows on the surface of the objects should not be removed. If multiple objects are present, keep them all; do not remove secondary objects. Maintain their relative scale and placement. Preserve any text that is printed on or is part of the main subject(s). For window objects, keep the content visible through the window; do not replace it with solid background. Do not stylize; preserve geometry, materials, and original colors. Preserve original color saturation and hue; do not increase saturation or shift hue. Replace the background with a plain solid neutral color with strong contrast. Remove only background content (scene context) and any background text, logos, or watermarks. Even, neutral lighting; crisp edges; high detail.
